channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs order imposing sanctions on International Criminal Court over investigations of Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order imposing sanctions on the International Criminal Court over investigations of Israel, a close U.S. ally. Neither the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Foster child death linked to state-contracted home prompts criminal investigation
item.description: The Greenville-based treatment center has closed after the death of an 11-year-old boy the day before Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Terri Langford, KPRC 2

item.title: Heartland lawmakers seek to fix 'fundamental' roadblock for farm kids seeking student aid
item.description: Sens. Joni Ernst, R-Iowa, and Michael Bennet, D-Colo., along with Rep. Tracey Mann, R-Kan, are sponsoring a bill to adjust the Free Application for Federal Student Aid qualification requirements to halt an unfair disadvantage for farm families.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: A federal judge in Boston will consider a request from 18 states to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump meets with congressional Republicans as GOP lawmakers argue over tax and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted an unusually long meeting with House Republicans at the White House on Thursday, turning over prime workspace for them to hammer out differences over the size, scope and details of their multitrillion-dollar plan to cut taxes, regulations and governmen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada consumers could get a break on egg prices
item.description: Just about everyone buys eggs, and higher prices haven't gone unnoticed in Carson City, where a law passed in 2021 is partly responsible for the eggflation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Dem stall tactics slow cabinet confirmations
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: Genesee County Chris Swanson has told The Associated Press he's launching a Democratic bid to be Michigan's next governor.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Rubio’s trip abroad was overshadowed by foreign policy at home, from USAID to Trump's Gaza plan
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio may have hoped to spend his first trip abroad focused exclusively on issues close to his heart: immigration, counternarcotics and curbing Chinese influence in the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US plans to seize Venezuelan president's plane held in Dominican Republic during Rubio visit
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Trump administration plans to seize a second plane belonging to Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro ’s government that is currently in the Dominican Republic. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas under partial hiring freeze as city readies for Badlands deal
item.description: Las Vegas expects a settlement soon in litigation over the Badlands golf course, and has already begun to freeze hiring to save money, according to city officials.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge pauses Trump federal worker buyouts
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday extended the deadline for federal workers to accept the Trump administration’s sweeping buyout offer. The court will weigh the merits of the bid to block the offer in a Monday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems, GOP spar on schools as Education Department hangs in the balance
item.description: The House Education and the Workforce Committee's first hearing of the year saw Democrats take on the "elephant in the room" as President Trump and Republicans seek to move toward abolishing the Education Department, though GOP lawmakers largely declined to address the issue.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: What is 'ordo amoris?' Vice President JD Vance invokes this medieval Catholic concept
item.description: Vice President JD Vance recently cited medieval Catholic theology in justifying the immigration crackdown under President Donald Trump. "Just google ‘ordo amoris,’” he posted Jan. 30 on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A short honeymoon for Catholics in D.C. as Vice President Vance clashes with bishops on migration
item.description: The sense of Catholic unity didn’t last long. Less than a week after Vice President JD Vance shared the inauguration stage with a senior Catholic cardinal, Vance picked a fight with the top American leaders of his church. The new vice president, a Catholic convert, accused the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters rally against Trump's stop-work order for USAID
item.description: USAID and nonprofit workers gathered near the Capitol in D.C. to protest Elon Musk's efforts to shut down the aid agency with his Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: NJ lawmakers create new bill to combat impersonating first responders
item.description: WEST WINDSOR, N.J. (PIX11) - First responders can be some of the most trustworthy figures in our communities, but some will try to impersonate them to commit other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: "With the support of President @realDonaldTrump, the @DOGE team will aim to make rapid safety upgrades to the air traffic control system," Musk wrote Wednesday on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats accuse DOGE of going after NOAA
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday accused Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) of improperly inserting itself into the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). In a joint statement, Reps. Zoe Lofgren (D-Calif.) and Jared Huffman (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic aid group downsizes by nearly 50% amid USAID freeze
item.description: The group started shutting down programs funded by USAID, which supplied about half of its $1.5 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE actions 'long overdue, much welcomed': House speaker
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is defending the Elon Musk-led DOGE, saying the government needs to spend money better.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Turner to lead HUD
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Scott Turner to lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), cementing another one of President Trump’s Cabinet choices. Senators voted 55-44 on Turner’s nomination. Sens. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) and Peter Welch (D-Vt.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans in Congress mostly shrug as Musk and DOGE set sights on spending
item.description: Congress controls the power of the purse, but Republicans on Capitol Hill have put up little resistance to efforts by the administration to suspend spending that they've already approved.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, MPR News

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, PIX 11
